title: citrus reticulata
intro: Citrus reticulata, also known as the mandarin or tangerine, is a small evergreen tree or shrub that belongs to the Rutaceae family and is native to China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial activities, and is traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of citrus reticulata include reducing stress, improving mood, and boosting the immune system, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Citrus reticulata is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as tea, essential oil, and capsules, which are available in health food stores and online.
